story: Smoke Monkey, the ruler of the Maya city Copán, threw open the doors of a brand new public meeting house. Called the Mat House, this special building welcomed people on June 12, 746. Imagine the excitement as citizens gathered to see the beautifully decorated space where important discussions and ceremonies would take place.
activity_question: What was the name of the new public building in Copán?
bonus_fact: The Maya used a complex calendar system to track important dates and events.

title: A City's New Name
story: Imagine a bustling harbor town getting a brand new name! That's exactly what happened to New Amsterdam in 1665. The English took control of this Dutch settlement and decided to rename it New York. Why? To honor the Duke of York, an important English nobleman. On June 12, the change became official, marking the start of a new chapter for this growing city.
activity_question: What was New Amsterdam renamed to?
follow_up_question: Can you guess which famous American city New York eventually became?

title: Daring Sea Capture in American Revolution
story: Cannons boomed as the American ship Unity chased the British vessel Margaretta. It was 1775, and the first naval battle of the American Revolution was underway on June 12. The brave colonists aboard Unity outmaneuvered their opponents, capturing the Margaretta in a thrilling victory at sea.
activity_question: What was the name of the American ship in this battle?
follow_up_question: Why do you think naval battles were important in the American Revolution?

title: Breathing Easy: The Gas Mask Invention
story: Imagine exploring dangerous places without fear! In 1849, Lewis Haslett from Louisville, Kentucky, made that possible. He patented a clever device on June 12 - the gas mask. This invention helps people breathe safely in harmful air. Firefighters, miners, and scientists still use versions of Haslett's idea today to stay safe while working.
activity_question: What did Lewis Haslett patent on June 12, 1849?
follow_up_question: Can you think of other jobs where gas masks might be important?

title: Egypt's Railway Revolution Begins
story: Choo-choo! In 1851, Egypt took a giant leap into the future. Abass I, the ruler of Egypt, signed a contract with Robert Louis Stevenson to build the country's first railway. This exciting project would change how people traveled across the land. On June 12, the wheels were set in motion for a transportation revolution in Africa.
activity_question: Who signed the contract to build Egypt's first railway?
bonus_fact: The first stage of this railway opened in 1854, connecting Alexandria to Kafer Eassa.
